text: In the last video, we saw the basics of file IO We saw how to do a basic bind and link in Python Why do we have to close the file? How to open the file? What is the open method? In today's video, we will continue this discussion and see some more interesting methods like readlines, writelines Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started Intro Sometimes there are many lines in our code and we want to read all those lines In this case, we can use the readlines method So, I will simply show you Let's make a myfile.txt first myfile.txt in which I am going to write Harry by Python course is great Let me correct its spelling Otherwise, people will think that I don't know its spelling which I know Now, I will tell you the usage of the readline method Why did I make the readline method? I made it so that you can read any file line by line It's simple You have written f is equal to open Now, what are you doing? You are reading a file till there is a line in it As simple as that At one time, I will do one thing I will print the line first So that at the end you can see the value of the line So, you can see the value of the line Now, I have to indent it What have I done? I should have done the indentation properly No problem, we all make mistakes So, you can see that Harry by Python course is great and then the line I printed is blank Now, I will do one thing I will print line and type of line and you can see the value of the line So, when I printed line and type of line It became an empty string At one time, it didn't get the line and at that time, we will stop reading it So, if I revert this program to this program So, you can see that we are reading line by line from myfile.txt But, there was only one line in myfile.txt So, it didn't feel good and I will write here and this umm tutorial is also awesome It's getting cold, my fingers are freezing Now, I will write here This is also good I am writing small, I don't feel like writing big But, it's not like that I don't feel like writing code When it's time to write code, fingers get cold You can see, what we are doing here We are printing line Harry by Python course is great Second line is printed Third line is printed If I want to do something where I have to read line by line Then, this is very handy We have to do this many times Sometimes, we need values which are in each line We have to parse them So, we can do that We are given a lot of data If you do machine learning data science Then, the data which is given to you on Kaggle etc is something like this I will show you a very good use case which will give you a lot of fun Suppose, I have 56, 45, 67 12, 34, 63 Similarly, I have this Suppose, this is someone's marks 3 marks of 3 students I have to write this properly I can do something like this I can say, I have this line Now, I will say M1 is equal to Let me correct the indent I have made double indent I will write F.split I will do something like this I will split it with comma I will say 0 Similarly, marks 2 and 3 will be done I will do 1, 2 I will do M2 I will do M3 After that, you can see M1, M2, M3 I will print them I will say, marks of student 1 in maths is
